name: Sev Khamani
description: A crumbly, tangy Gujarati snack made from cooked chana dal, tempered with mustard seeds and topped with sev.
steps: Grind soaked chana dal with green chilli and ginger into a coarse paste without water.
Steam the paste in a greased plate for 12–15 minutes until cooked through. Let cool and crumble.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add turmeric and pour over crumbled dal.
Mix in lemon juice, sugar, and salt. Toss well.
Top with sev, pomegranate seeds, and fresh coriander. Serve immediately.

name: Idli Vada Sambhar
description: Fluffy steamed idlis and crispy medu vadas served with a robust toor dal sambhar and fresh coconut chutney.
steps: Pour idli batter into greased moulds and steam for 10–12 minutes. Rest for 2 minutes before unmoulding.
Grind soaked urad dal into a thick, airy batter. Season with salt. Shape into rings and deep fry until golden.
Pressure cook toor dal with tomatoes and turmeric. Mash well.
In a pan, temper mustard seeds, red chilli, and curry leaves. Add onion, sambhar powder, tamarind water, and cooked dal. Simmer 10 minutes.
Serve hot idlis and vadas with sambhar and coconut chutney on the side.

name: Ragi Dhokla
description: A nutritious twist on classic dhokla using ragi (finger millet) flour, lightly spiced and steamed to a spongy finish.
steps: Mix ragi flour, besan, yoghurt, green chilli paste, ginger paste, salt, and lemon juice into a smooth batter. Rest 5 minutes.
Add eno and mix quickly. Pour into a greased steaming plate immediately.
Steam for 15–18 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool slightly and cut into squares.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds, sesame seeds, and curry leaves. Pour over dhokla.
Serve warm with green chutney.

name: Poha
description: Light, fluffy flattened rice tempered with mustard seeds, curry leaves, onion and peas — a classic Maharashtrian breakfast.
steps: Rinse poha in a colander under running water until soft. Drain and season with turmeric, sugar, and salt. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pan, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add green chilli and onion, sauté until translucent.
Add green peas and cook 2 minutes.
Add poha and toss gently on low heat for 3–4 minutes.
Finish with lemon juice, fresh coriander, and a handful of sev. Serve hot.

name: Upma
description: Savoury roasted semolina cooked with vegetables and tempered with mustard seeds, curry leaves and green chilli.
steps: Dry roast semolina on medium heat until fragrant and lightly golden. Set aside.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, chana dal, and urad dal until golden. Add curry leaves, green chilli, ginger, and onion. Sauté until soft.
Add vegetables and cook 3 minutes.
Pour in water, bring to a boil, and season with salt.
Add roasted semolina in a steady stream, stirring constantly. Cover and cook on low for 5 minutes. Fluff and serve.

name: Adai
description: Thick, protein-rich lentil and rice crepes from Tamil Nadu, spiced with dry red chillies and curry leaves.
steps: Soak rice and all dals together for 3–4 hours.
Grind with dry red chillies, ginger, and asafoetida to a slightly coarse, thick batter. Do not over-grind.
Mix in chopped onion, curry leaves, and salt.
Heat a cast iron tawa, pour a ladle of batter and spread into a thick circle. Drizzle oil around edges.
Cook on medium heat until edges crisp. Flip and cook 2 minutes. Serve with avial or coconut chutney.

name: Pesarattu
description: Andhra-style whole green moong crepes that are naturally protein-packed, crispy on the edges and served with ginger chutney.
steps: Drain soaked moong dal and grind with green chilli, ginger, cumin, and a little water into a smooth batter.
Mix in chopped onion, coriander, and salt.
Heat a non-stick tawa, pour batter and spread thin. Drizzle oil around edges.
Cook until edges turn golden and crisp, flip and cook 1 minute.
Serve hot with ginger chutney or upma stuffed inside.

name: Uttapam
description: Thick, soft rice and lentil pancakes topped with onion, tomato, green chilli, and coriander — the comfort dosa.
steps: Season dosa batter lightly with salt if needed.
Heat a tawa on medium, pour a thick ladle of batter. Do not spread too thin.
Immediately scatter onion, tomato, green chilli, carrot, and coriander over the top. Press gently.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cover and cook 3–4 minutes until base is golden.
Flip carefully and cook 2 minutes. Serve with coconut chutney and sambhar.

name: Paneer Paratha
description: Flaky whole wheat flatbreads stuffed with spiced crumbled paneer, cooked on a tawa with generous ghee.
steps: Knead flour with water and a pinch of salt into a smooth, pliable dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Mix crumbled paneer with green chilli, ginger, cumin, amchur, coriander, and salt.
Roll dough into a disc, place filling in centre, seal edges, and roll again gently into a flat circle.
Cook on a hot tawa with ghee on both sides until golden patches appear.
Serve hot with pickle, yoghurt, and butter.

name: Bombay Sandwich
description: Mumbai's iconic layered sandwich with green chutney, spiced potato, cucumber, tomato, and beet — toasted crispy.
steps: Butter all bread slices on one side. Spread green chutney generously on the other side.
Layer potato slices on first slice, sprinkle sandwich masala and salt.
Add cucumber, tomato, and beetroot. Top with second slice chutney-side down.
Press in a sandwich toaster or on a tawa with butter until golden and crisp.
Cut into triangles. Serve with extra chutney and tomato ketchup.

name: Masala Dosa
description: Paper-thin crispy rice and lentil crepes filled with spiced potato masala, served with coconut chutney and sambhar.
steps: Prepare potato masala: temper mustard seeds, urad dal, curry leaves. Add onion and green chilli, sauté. Mix in mashed potato with turmeric and salt.
Heat a cast iron tawa until very hot. Sprinkle water to cool slightly.
Pour a ladle of batter and spread in a thin outward circle immediately.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until edges lift and base is golden-crisp.
Place potato filling in the centre, fold dosa over it. Serve with coconut chutney and sambhar.

name: Appe
description: Bite-sized, crispy-outside soft-inside dosa batter balls cooked in a paniyaram pan with minimal oil.
steps: Mix onion, green chilli, coriander, carrot, and curry leaves into the batter. Adjust salt.
Heat paniyaram (appe) pan and add a few drops of oil in each cavity.
Pour batter into each cavity until three-quarters full.
Cover and cook on low-medium heat for 3–4 minutes until base is crisp.
Flip each appe with a skewer and cook the other side 2 minutes. Serve with coconut chutney.

name: Handwa
description: A savoury Gujarati baked cake made from fermented rice and lentil batter loaded with bottle gourd and sesame seeds.
steps: Mix handwa flour with yoghurt, grated lauki, ginger-chilli paste, turmeric, red chilli, and salt into a thick batter.
Add eno and mix quickly. Batter will become airy.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan with lid, splutter mustard seeds and sesame seeds.
Pour batter in. Sprinkle more sesame on top. Cover and cook on low heat 20 minutes.
Flip carefully and cook other side 15 minutes until both sides are golden. Cut into wedges and serve.

name: Vermicelli Upma
description: Roasted semiya (vermicelli) cooked with vegetables, tempered with mustard and curry leaves into a quick, light breakfast.
steps: Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add green chilli and onion, sauté until soft.
Add carrot and peas. Cook 3 minutes.
Pour in water, bring to a boil. Season with salt.
Add vermicelli, stir and cover. Cook on low heat 5–6 minutes until water is absorbed.
Fluff with a fork, add lemon juice, and serve warm.

name: Avocado Toast
description: Creamy spiced avocado on sourdough with a poached egg, chilli flakes, and a squeeze of lime — elevated yet simple.
steps: Toast sourdough until golden and crisp.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, cumin, chilli flakes, salt, and pepper.
Poach egg in gently simmering water with a splash of vinegar for 3 minutes (optional).
Spread avocado mixture generously on toast.
Top with poached egg, cherry tomatoes, and a drizzle of olive oil.

name: Besan ka Chilla
description: Crispy chickpea flour pancakes spiced with ajwain and green chilli — high protein, quick, and deeply satisfying.
steps: Whisk besan with water into a smooth, lump-free batter of pouring consistency.
Mix in onion, tomato, green chilli, coriander, ajwain, turmeric, red chilli, and salt.
Heat a non-stick pan on medium, pour a ladle of batter and spread into a thin circle.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until edges lift and base is golden.
Flip and cook other side 2 minutes. Serve with green chutney or yoghurt.

name: Masala Omelette
description: Fluffy Indian-style omelette loaded with onion, green chilli, tomato, and coriander — a street-stall classic.
steps: Whisk eggs with turmeric, red chilli, salt, and pepper until frothy.
Mix in onion, tomato, green chilli, and coriander.
Heat butter in a pan over medium heat until foamy.
Pour egg mixture and swirl to cover pan. Let edges set for 1 minute.
Fold in half once the centre is just set. Slide onto a plate and serve with toast.

name: Fluffy Pancakes
description: Light, golden buttermilk pancakes with a hint of vanilla, served with honey, fresh fruit, or maple syrup.
steps: Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl, whisk egg, buttermilk, melted butter, and vanilla.
Fold wet into dry ingredients until just combined — lumps are fine, do not over-mix.
Heat a non-stick pan on medium-low, pour a ladle of batter. Cook until bubbles form on surface and edges look set.
Flip once and cook 1 minute. Serve stacked with honey, banana slices, or maple syrup.
